name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD chemist/materials scientist with 6+ years in analytical characterization, method troubleshooting, DOE-guided process development, and laboratory quality systems. Uses LC-MS, GC-MS, NMR/GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA/DSC, QCM-D, and electrochemistry to support robust methods, investigations, and reproducible material-quality decisions.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Advisor: Prof. Seth Marder
(Transferred from Georgia Institute of Technology)

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Lead DOE-guided bio-based polymer process development, converting analytical data and material-quality metrics into reproducible process windows, specifications, and troubleshooting plans.
Designed and characterized organic molecular materials across 15+ variants using LC-MS/GC-MS-adjacent workflows, NMR/GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA/DSC, and electrochemistry.
Developed thin-film process controls and surface-preparation protocols, linking process conditions to morphology, interface quality, and analytical performance readouts.
Optimized CVD polymer thin-film deposition with DOE and QCM-D monitoring, establishing process-control parameters and repeatable characterization workflows.
Managed characterization/process-development lab operations for a 15-member group; implemented equipment tracking, calibration, SOP, and safety systems that improved instrument uptime by 30%.

projects.title: Analytical Characterization & Method Robustness
projects.description: Used orthogonal analytical methods to connect structure, purity, degradation, and material performance across organic material variants.
projects.tech: LC-MS, GC-MS, NMR, GPC, FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA/DSC

projects.title: Bio-Based Polymer Process Controls
projects.description: Defined DOE-guided process windows and material-quality specifications for reproducible polymer scale-up.
projects.tech: DOE, SPC, polymer characterization, technical reports

projects.title: AFOSR Thin-Film Process Development
projects.description: Linked CVD/surface preparation conditions to film morphology, interface quality, and optical performance.
projects.tech: CVD, QCM-D, surface prep, thin films

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Beyond Rhodamine B: Investigating Alternative Xanthene Cores for Modular Electrochromic Systems." (Manuscript under preparation).
conferences: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials, ACS Fall National Meeting, Denver, CO, August 2024.
Electrochemical and Spectroscopic Properties of Substituted Rhodamine Derivatives, Gerischer-Lewerenz Electrochemistry Symposium, Fort Collins, CO, August 2024.
Decoupling Redox and Color for High-Contrast Electrochromics, Excited State Processes Conference, Santa Fe, NM, June 2023.
Electrochromism: Decoupling Color and Redox in Rhodamine-Based Systems, Rocky Mountain Solid State Chemistry Workshop, Boulder, CO, January 2023.

skills.Chromatography & MS: LC-MS, GC-MS, HPLC/GC-adjacent method troubleshooting, sample preparation, NMR, GPC/SEC
skills.Materials & Stability: FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, DSC, QCM-D, cyclic voltammetry, degradation/failure analysis, structure-property relationships
skills.Process & Quality Systems: DOE, SPC, JMP (basic), Python (basic), Excel, SOPs, calibration systems, technical reports, reproducibility studies

honors: Plastic Sustainability Innovation in Material Award

teaching.role: Teaching Assistant, Organic Chemistry I & Lab
teaching.description: Mentored 120+ undergraduates; developed supplemental materials improving exam performance by 15%.
